summary: Jon Jones closed distance behind long oblique kicks, secured a clinch against the fence and unloaded elbows that opened a cut, forcing an early stoppage. Gane never found the range to use his kickboxing.
Jones used his reach advantage to pepper Cormier from the outside for five rounds, mixing spinning strikes with clinch control whenever Cormier tried to close the gap.
In his toughest fight, Jones was hurt multiple times by Gustafsson's boxing and had to rely on late clinch work and a takedown to close the scorecards in his favor.
Aspinall slipped a takedown attempt, countered with a short right hand and finished with ground strikes in under 20 seconds, showing elite takedown defense reflexes for a heavyweight.
After a scramble on the mat, Aspinall transitioned smoothly into a kimura, underlining his black-belt grappling even when the fight unexpectedly hit the floor.
A knee injury early in the fight ended the contest before real data on his cardio past the first minutes could be gathered.
In a kickboxing super-fight, Doumbe used lateral movement and check hooks to control range over five rounds, rarely engaging in exchanges he did not initiate.
A southpaw left straight dropped his opponent after a probing first round; Doumbe rarely commits to combinations, preferring single heavy counters.
Pettis famously used the cage as a launchpad for a kick before eventually locking a guillotine, showing his signature creativity under pressure.
Chandler's early pressure and a check hook caught Pettis clean before he could establish range, ending the fight in under a minute.
Alvarez rushed the striking exchanges early and Pettis's chin was tested and found wanting in the opening minute.
An all-out firefight ended when Gaethje landed a check-hook counter, showing his fight-ending power but also his willingness to trade even when hurt.
Once Khabib closed distance and got the takedown against the fence, Gaethje had no answer on the mat and was submitted in the second round.
Gaethje pressed forward aggressively but was caught in a guillotine during a scramble, again showing that elite grapplers who close distance are his biggest threat.
After being dropped and badly hurt in round one, Oliveira recovered, took the fight to the mat and finished with a choke, proving both his chin's fragility and his mental toughness.
Makhachev out-wrestled Oliveira and, in a rare turn, out-grappled him on the mat as well, showing that Oliveira can be beaten at his own game by a superior wrestler-grappler.
Khabib absorbed early striking, closed distance repeatedly, and once against the fence there was no takedown defense that could keep him off top position for long.
Relentless double-leg attempts eventually landed; once on top, Khabib's ground-and-pound opened the choke.
Makhachev used sustained clinch pressure against the fence to control output for most rounds against a superior pure striker.
After a takedown, Makhachev methodically advanced position until an arm-triangle choke closed the show, a common finishing pattern for him.
Pereira's left hook found the target after a feint sequence; his kickboxing power carried a clear advantage in the pure striking exchanges.
A single counter right hand ended the fight quickly, continuing a pattern of first- and second-round finishes and limited data past round two.
Adesanya adjusted his gameplan with a check hook counter, showing he can beat Pereira in a rematch when he avoids standing directly in punching range.
Strickland's constant forward pressure and volume boxing denied Adesanya the space he needs to counter-strike effectively.
